findings.evidence: `GroupAlgebraProduct.lean:177` declares `theorem prodPointsMulEquiv_mapValue` without an attribute; existing analogous APIs use simp, e.g. `DiagonalizableGroup.lean:196 @[simp] theorem pointsMulEquiv_mapValue` and `SplitTorus.lean:101 @[simp] theorem pointsMulEquiv_mapValue`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to both value-naturality lemmas so `simp` rewrites product points after `AlgHom.mapValue` componentwise.
findings.issue: `DiagonalizableGroup.prodPointsMulEquiv_mapValue` (and the parallel `MonoidAlgebra.prodPointsMulEquiv_mapValue` at line 118) is a normal-form naturality lemma but is not marked `@[simp]`.

findings.evidence: `GroupAlgebraProduct.lean:187` declares the compatibility theorem without `@[simp]`; the directly analogous functoriality compatibility is simp, `DiagonalizableGroupFunctoriality.lean:129 @[simp] theorem pointsMulEquiv_pointsMap`.
findings.fix: Mark it `@[simp]` so the product-points equivalence rewrites automatically under `DiagonalizableGroup.pointsMulEquiv` to the coproduct of the two restricted characters.
findings.issue: `pointsMulEquiv_prodPointsMulEquiv` is the characteristic compatibility with the character description, but it is not a simp lemma.

summary: The exposed equivalences and generator lemmas are scoped appropriately, but two characteristic product-point lemmas are missing the simp annotations expected for this API.
